name: Chakra UI runtime tradeoffs
description: CSS-in-JS runtime footprint (emotion + styled-system): when it matters, what to watch, and how to decide.

metadata.intent: Evaluate Chakra’s runtime overhead vs alternatives for performance-sensitive apps

must_use: Measure or at least reason about high-frequency re-render areas
must_not: Adopt Chakra blindly for extremely performance-sensitive, high-frequency UIs without comparing alternatives
conventions: Most small/medium data-driven apps are fine; hotspots (huge tables, realtime dashboards) require extra care.

steps.description: Cause: Chakra uses CSS-in-JS (emotion) and styled-system style computations; this adds runtime work (style prop resolution, class generation).
It matters most when: UI re-renders frequently (high-frequency data), large lists/tables update often, or you have strict performance budgets.

Decision rule: if your product is heavy on dynamic dashboards and large data grids, compare Chakra with utility-first CSS or source-owned systems. If you’re shipping small/medium apps and value DX + theming, Chakra is often a good fit.
